threats.title: Yield Liquidation Completely Broken Due to msg.sender Account Resolution
threats.hypothesis_description: The botAdmin cannot liquidate unhealthy yield positions because the `_unstake` and `_repay` functions incorrectly resolve the active yield account using `yieldAccounts[msg.sender]` instead of the user's stored `sd.yieldAccount`. When the bot calls these functions, it attempts to use its own non-existent or empty yield account, causing the transaction to revert.
threats.investigation_rationale: Liquidation is the core solvency mechanism for overcollateralized lending. If the bot cannot force-unstake and repay underwater positions, the protocol will accumulate unrecoverable bad debt as yield token values fluctuate.

proof_of_signal.pattern: vars.yieldAccout = IYieldAccount(yieldAccounts[msg.sender]); used instead of sd.yieldAccount

preconditions: A user's yield staking position becomes unhealthy (health factor < unstakeHeathFactor)
botAdmin attempts to call unstake or repay to liquidate the position

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: yieldAccounts[msg.sender] evaluates to address(0) for the botAdmin
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Users can take maximum leverage knowing they can never be liquidated
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attackers extract value by borrowing max WETH against yield tokens; if yield token value drops, they abandon the position, leaving the protocol with bad debt.

economic_questions: Can any other role successfully liquidate the position?
Does the revert happen at the YieldStakingBase level or the external protocol level?
validation_requirements: Simulate a botAdmin call to unstake and verify the revert
Calculate the maximum bad debt accumulation based on current caps

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to open a max-leverage yield position
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Equal to the drop in collateral value below the debt amount

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Sufficient lending pool liquidity to borrow against the NFT
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Prior to a market downturn in the yield token
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Directly creates bad debt in the lending pool

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional loss of principal due to bad debt
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire yield borrowing cap across all assets

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if botAdmin has a yield account initialized (it still wouldn't hold the user's assets)

threats.title: Yield Borrowing Cap Inflated by Using Borrow Index Instead of Supply Index
threats.hypothesis_description: In `YieldLogic.executeYieldBorrowERC20`, the total supply used to enforce the `yieldCap` is calculated by multiplying the scaled supply by `groupData.borrowIndex` instead of `assetData.supplyIndex`. Because the borrow index compounds faster than the supply index, the calculated total supply is artificially inflated over time, allowing yield managers to bypass the intended cap.
threats.investigation_rationale: Yield caps are critical risk management parameters to limit protocol exposure to external yield strategies. Inflating this cap allows disproportionate risk concentration.
preconditions: Protocol has been running long enough for borrowIndex to diverge significantly from supplyIndex
Yield manager attempts to borrow up to the cap

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: borrowIndex grows faster than supplyIndex due to interest spread and protocol fees
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Yield stakers can extract more liquidity from the pool than the admin-configured risk limits allow, increasing their yield farming profits at the expense of pool safety.

economic_questions: How fast does the borrowIndex diverge from the supplyIndex under normal utilization?
What is the maximum excess borrowing possible after 1 year?
validation_requirements: Calculate the index divergence over time using the configured DefaultInterestRateModel
Test borrowing beyond the actual supply cap in a simulated environment

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient NFT collateral to support the excess borrowing
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Yield spread on the excess borrowed amount
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: External yield rate minus borrow rate on the excess capital

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires available liquidity in the lending pool
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After significant protocol runtime when index divergence is large
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Reduces available liquidity for normal withdrawals

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Lending pool depositors (increased risk, potential withdrawal DoS)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The difference between the inflated cap and the true cap

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Compare borrowRate vs supplyRate in configured IRMs
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Quantify the exact inflation multiplier (borrowIndex / supplyIndex)

threats.title: Users Can Prevent Yield Liquidation by Revoking WETH Approval
threats.hypothesis_description: During `YieldStakingBase._repay`, if the claimed yield is insufficient to cover the debt plus the unstake fine, the contract attempts to pull the shortfall (`extraAmount`) directly from the NFT owner's wallet using `safeTransferFrom`. A malicious user can intentionally revoke their WETH approval or empty their WETH balance, causing the botAdmin's liquidation transaction to revert.
threats.investigation_rationale: A liquidation mechanism must never rely on the cooperation or token approvals of the user being liquidated. This allows users to opt-out of liquidation.
preconditions: A yield position is underwater (claimed yield < debt + fine)
botAdmin attempts to liquidate via repay
User has 0 WETH balance or 0 WETH allowance to the YieldStakingBase contract

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: vars.extraAmount = vars.nftDebtWithFine - vars.claimedYield; followed by safeTransferFrom
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: User controls the success of the liquidation transaction via their WETH allowance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: User avoids paying the shortfall and fine, forcing the protocol to absorb the bad debt while they keep their other assets safe.

economic_questions: Can the protocol seize the NFT if the repay fails?
Is there any fallback liquidation method?
validation_requirements: Simulate a liquidation where the user has revoked approval
Verify that the NFT remains locked and debt remains active

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Saves the user from paying the extraAmount shortfall

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total value of the shortfall across all underwater positions

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if the protocol has any alternative way to clear the debt without pulling from the user
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm extraAmount logic triggers when yield drops

threats.title: Unstake Fine is Economically Negligible Due to uint16 Type and Direct Addition
threats.hypothesis_description: The `maxUnstakeFine` is defined as a `uint16`, limiting its maximum value to 65,535. In `_repay`, this fine is added directly to the debt (`vars.nftDebt + sd.unstakeFine`), which is denominated in WETH (18 decimals). A maximum fine of 65,535 wei is worth a fraction of a cent, completely breaking the economic penalty for liquidation.
threats.investigation_rationale: Without a meaningful liquidation penalty, users have free optionality. They can take maximum leverage and simply let the bot liquidate them if the market drops, socializing their losses without penalty.
deployment_assumptions: Underlying asset has 18 decimals (e.g., WETH, DAI)
threats.config_evidence: InitConfigYield.s.sol line 84: yieldEthStakingLido.setNftUnstakeParams(address(addrWPUNK), 100, 1.05e18); sets fine to 100 wei.

proof_of_signal.pattern: vars.nftDebtWithFine = vars.nftDebt + sd.unstakeFine; (where unstakeFine is bounded by uint16 max 65535)

preconditions: Admin configures maxUnstakeFine within uint16 limits

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Adding a uint16 value directly to an 18-decimal wad value
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Users face no economic penalty for being liquidated
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Users can treat the protocol as a risk-free options contract: keep the yield if it goes up, abandon the position to the bot if it goes down, paying only ~65,535 wei as a penalty.

economic_questions: Was the fine intended to be a percentage (e.g., in basis points)?
How does the lack of penalty affect borrower behavior?
validation_requirements: Verify the exact wei amount added to the debt during a simulated bot liquidation

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to open a yield position
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Value of the avoided liquidation penalty (e.g., 5-10% of position size)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High ROI on the avoided risk

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During high volatility in yield token prices

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Protocol revenue (lost fines) and lending pool (increased bad debt risk)
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of intended protocol fee revenue

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check deployment scripts for intended fine values
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm the addition is direct and not scaled by 1e14 or similar

threats.title: Missing Staleness Check Allows Borrowing Against Outdated Oracle Prices
threats.hypothesis_description: `PriceOracle.getAssetPriceFromChainlink` verifies that `updatedAt != 0` and `answeredInRound >= roundId`, but fails to verify that the price was updated recently (`block.timestamp - updatedAt < MAX_DELAY`). If a Chainlink feed stops updating, the protocol will continue to accept the stale price indefinitely.
threats.investigation_rationale: Oracle staleness is a classic DeFi vulnerability. If a feed halts during a market crash, attackers can deposit the crashed asset and borrow against its old, higher price, stealing funds from the protocol.
preconditions: A Chainlink oracle feed stops updating (heartbeat failure or sequencer downtime)
The actual market price of the asset drops significantly below the stale oracle price

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Accepting any updatedAt > 0 regardless of current block.timestamp
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Exploiting the discrepancy between the stale on-chain price and the real market price
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker buys the crashed asset cheaply on a DEX, deposits it into the protocol, and borrows a stablecoin at the inflated stale valuation, effectively selling the asset above market price.

economic_questions: Which assets have the highest risk of oracle halts?
What is the maximum LTV for these assets?
validation_requirements: Simulate an oracle halt and a subsequent market price drop
Calculate the profit margin based on asset LTVs

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to purchase the crashed asset on external markets
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Profitable if market price drops below (Stale Price * LTV)
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Executed while the oracle is stalled

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires sufficient stablecoin liquidity in the lending pool to borrow
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after a severe market crash that halts the oracle
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Drains stable liquidity, leaving the protocol with undercollateralized bad debt

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Available liquidity of the borrowed asset

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no upstream staleness checks exist in the protocol

threats.title: Yield Account Permanently Bricked if External Yield Token Loses Value
threats.hypothesis_description: In `YieldSavingsDai`, `getAccountTotalUnstakedYield` calculates the available balance by subtracting `accountYieldInWithdraws` from the current sDAI balance. If the external sDAI balance drops (e.g., due to MakerDAO slashing or negative DSR) after a user has initiated an unstake, the balance may fall below the recorded `inWithdraw` amount. This causes the subtraction to underflow and revert, permanently bricking the yield account.
threats.investigation_rationale: While sDAI rarely goes down in value, relying on external balances to always strictly exceed historically recorded withdrawal amounts creates a fragility that can permanently lock user accounts if the assumption is violated.

proof_of_signal.pattern: return balance - inWithdraw; (where balance is external and inWithdraw is historical)

preconditions: User initiates an unstake, recording a specific inWithdraw amount
The external sDAI exchange rate drops before the repay is executed
The new sDAI balance is less than the recorded inWithdraw amount

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: balance - inWithdraw without safe math fallback if balance drops
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: External protocol value loss triggers internal accounting reverts
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Not directly profitable for an attacker, but causes severe DoS and potential loss of funds for the victim whose account is bricked.

economic_questions: Can the user recover their remaining funds if this underflow occurs?
Does this affect Lido and EtherFi implementations as well?
validation_requirements: Simulate a negative rebase or exchange rate drop in the external protocol
Attempt to call stake or unstake on the affected account

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Locks user funds and prevents further protocol usage for that account

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users with active unstake requests during a slashing event
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete lock of remaining yield assets in that account

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm Solidity 0.8+ reverts on the subtraction

threats.title: Admin Can Drain All User Yield Assets via rescue Function
threats.hypothesis_description: `YieldRegistry.rescue` allows the `POOL_ADMIN` to execute arbitrary calls from any `YieldAccount` via `YieldAccount.rescue`. Since the `YieldAccount` holds the user's actual yield-bearing assets (e.g., stETH, eETH, sDAI), a compromised or malicious admin can use this function to transfer all staked assets from every user to themselves.
threats.investigation_rationale: While protected by an admin modifier, this represents a complete centralization risk and rug-pull vector. The `rescue` function is overly permissive by allowing arbitrary `functionCall` execution rather than restricted token sweeping.
preconditions: POOL_ADMIN private key is compromised, or the admin acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Arbitrary execution from a contract holding user funds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin crafts calldata to call `transfer` on the yield tokens (stETH, etc.)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of the yield assets deposited by all users across the protocol.

economic_questions: Is there a timelock on the POOL_ADMIN role?
Are there any off-chain monitoring systems that would catch this?
validation_requirements: Verify that the POOL_ADMIN can craft a payload to transfer stETH out of a YieldAccount

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas cost for the rescue calls
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 100% of Total Value Locked in Yield Staking

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When TVL is at its peak
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete destruction of protocol trust and loss of all user funds

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire TVL of the yield staking modules

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Review the multi-sig or timelock setup for the POOL_ADMIN role in deployment scripts
